name: Depth Anything (Monocular Depth Estimation)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); TikTok / ByteDance; HuggingFace Spaces available

auth.notes: Local inference via HuggingFace transformers; no auth

performance.notes: GPU required for real-time; ~20fps on A100 at 518px; V2 small model is fast on consumer GPUs

agent_readiness.notes: SOTA monocular depth estimation model. V2 improvements over V1: cleaner training data, better detail preservation, metric depth for real-world scale. Four sizes: Small, Base, Large, Giant. Outputs relative or metric depth map. Available via HuggingFace Transformers (AutoModelForDepthEstimation). Applications: 3D scene understanding, autonomous driving, robotic grasping, image editing (background blur, relighting), AR/VR. Works with single RGB image.

gotchas: Relative depth (V1) vs metric depth (V2) — different use cases
GPU strongly recommended for practical speeds
Outdoor/indoor domain shift can affect quality
best_when: Monocular depth estimation for scene understanding
Background blur / defocus effects from single images
Autonomous systems needing depth from cameras
avoid_when: Precise metric depth needed for robotics (LiDAR/stereo more reliable)
Real-time mobile/edge deployment (too large for mobile)
Transparent or reflective surfaces (depth estimation fails)
summary: Depth Anything V2 is state-of-the-art monocular depth estimation from ByteDance/TikTok. Outputs relative or metric depth maps from single RGB images. Four model sizes (Small→Giant). Available via HuggingFace Transformers. Used for background blur, 3D scene understanding, robotic grasping, AR/VR, and autonomous driving perception. V2 adds cleaner training data and metric depth support.
